76		}

11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and
25	--model as opaque passthrough constraints forwarded to the agent unchanged.
26	DDx does not validate these values or branch on them; the agent owns routing
27	within the requested power bounds.
28	
29	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
30	  drained     — no ready beads remain
31	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
32	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
33	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
34	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
35	
36	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
37	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
38	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
39	`,
40			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit
41	  ddx work
42	
43	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
44	  ddx work --once
45	
46	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
47	  ddx work --poll-interval 30s
48	
49	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
50	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
51	
52	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
53	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
54			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
55			RunE: f.runWork,
56		}
57	
58		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
59		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
60		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
61		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
62		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")

DDx's cli/internal/agent/agent_catalog_shared.go reads ~/.config/agent/models.yaml directly and hard-codes surface translations like "agent.openai -> embedded-openai". That's a consumer baking in agent's catalog schema + internal surface naming. When agent renames a surface or restructures surface_policy (as v0.7.0 did), every consumer breaks.

With profile-owned placement policy (per agent-285a05d2), the catalog now carries placement, cost ceilings, failure policy, and reasoning defaults per profile/target. Consumers need to resolve a profile name to something routable — but they shouldn't be the ones reading YAML to do it.

## Proposal

Service exposes catalog + profile projection:

- Service.ListProfiles(ctx) -> []ProfileInfo  (name, description, deprecated alias-of, provenance)
- Service.ResolveProfile(ctx, name) -> ResolvedProfile (candidate surfaces + models + placement hint + cost ceiling + reasoning default + failure policy)
- Service.ProfileAliases(ctx) -> map[oldName]newName  (for deprecated aliases like the v0.6.0 harness-flavored preset removals — consumer gets a clear migration signal)

Consumers never touch ~/.config/agent/models.yaml, never know "agent.openai" surface naming.

## Consumer impact

Shrinks DDx ddx-e7254128 post-v0.7.0 catalog follow-up, ddx-ac5c7fdb (thin-consumer epic), ddx-bbb65768 (profile automation default), ddx-4b59b816 (cost-tiered routing lookups).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=standard harness= model= probe=no viable provider

/home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/fizeau@v0.10.4/.ddx/beads.jsonl:125:{"acceptance":"1. Service final events expose normalized final response text through a stable public field or public method; old raw text_delta behavior remains available for one migration window.
2. Native Service.Execute maps agent result output to the normalized final text.
3. Codex, Claude, and at least one of pi, opencode, or gemini map existing stream aggregate final text into the normalized final text without exposing raw stream-frame envelopes.
4. A DDx-style reviewer verdict extraction regression parses APPROVE/BLOCK text from the normalized field and does not inspect raw codex item.completed JSON.
5. CONTRACT-003 documents the field or method plus migration guidance.
6. go test . ./internal/harnesses/... passes.","claimed-at":"2026-04-20T15:19:12Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1088681","closing_commit_sha":"3c92cf2031e45fbf155db020aea96ca694cd6f48","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:22:25.908512045Z","description":"Surfaced by DDx consumer ddx-f7ae036f. DDx's post-merge reviewer currently parses raw harness stream frames to extract the reviewer's final verdict text. For codex that means reading `{"type":"item.completed", ...}` envelopes directly. That's agent-owned stream format; consumers shouldn't depend on it.

## Concrete failure

DDx's verdict-extraction regex anchors on the reviewer's text, but the reviewer's text is embedded inside codex's stream-frame JSON. When the frame shape shifts (v0.6.0→v0.7.0, or per-harness variation), the regex silently misses and ParseReviewVerdict defaults to BLOCK. An APPROVE-verdicted review gets recorded as BLOCK; the bead reopens; the loop re-runs it; the reviewer APPROVEs again; same silent mis-extract. Cost-visible wasted throughput.

## Proposal

Expose the final cleaned response text from the harness at the service boundary. Two possible shapes — pick whichever fits the existing ExecuteResponse contract:

1. `ExecuteResponse.FinalText string` — the harness's user-facing final message, already stripped of stream-frame wrapping, reasoning blocks, and tool-call scaffolding.
2. New service method `Service.FinalResponse(sessionID) (string, error)` that reads from the session log and returns the normalized text.

Per-harness responsibility:
- codex: strip `{"type":"item.completed"}` envelopes, concatenate `content` fields.
- claude: last message.content in the assistant turn.
- gemini / opencode / pi: per their respective stream shapes.

DDx then parses verdict text from FinalText, not from raw frames. No consumer-side per-harness stream knowledge.

## Boundary argument

Agent owns harness dispatch and stream format (CONTRACT-003). Anything consumer-side that does `if response.startsWith('{"type":"item.')` is patching around agent ownership. This fix moves the stream-format concern back behind the service boundary where it belongs.

## Out of scope

- Tool-call output handling — separate concern.
- Context-overflow handling inside the native loop — DDx treats that as non-terminal; if agent's native loop emits context-overflow as a recoverable final response shape, consumers will see it via FinalText naturally.","events":[{

# Investigation: bare `ddx work` and "no viable routing candidate: N candidates rejected"

Bead: ddx-9e4c238d
Base rev: 9f7d893bff9d4788459f259b6ff5e83c96ad430b

## Summary

The premise in the bead's description is incorrect: **DDx does not pre-filter
routing candidates for bare `ddx work`**. The error
`no viable routing candidate: N candidates rejected` originates inside
fizeau's routing engine, not DDx. The fix called for in AC#2 ("Bare `ddx
work` (no pins) passes a maximally-permissive ServiceExecuteRequest to
fizeau") is already in effect at base rev 9f7d893b. Existing tests
prove it.

## File:line walk-through (AC#1)

The bead lists four hypotheses for "where DDx rejects 4 candidates". Each
is wrong on the merits at this revision:

### Hypothesis 1 — `ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService`

`cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:392-434`
```
func ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(ctx context.Context, workDir, harnessName, model, provider, modelRef string) error {
    if harnessName == "" {
        return nil
    }
    ...
}
```

Returns `nil` immediately when `harnessName == ""` (no operator pin).

Caller in `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1652-1656`:
```go
// Skipped for ddx work (treatPassthroughAsOpaque=true): harness/provider/model
// are opaque passthrough constraints there; DDx does not validate them.
if !treatPassthroughAsOpaque {
    if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(...); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("execute-loop: %w", err)
    }
}
```

`ddx work` invokes `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true)` (`cli/cmd/work.go:92`),
so `treatPassthroughAsOpaque == true` and the call is skipped entirely. Even
if it did run, the `harnessName == ""` short-circuit would still return nil.

### Hypothesis 2 — `escalation.InferTier`

`cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go` defines escalation, and
`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1865-1872` invokes `escalation.InferTier(b)` purely to
choose a tier name passed forward as `attemptProfile` (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1779).
There is no candidate filter or rejection logic here — the inferred tier
becomes a `Profile` field in `ServiceExecuteRequest`. Unmatched profiles
are fizeau's concern, not DDx's.

### Hypothesis 3 — `ServiceExecuteRequest` construction

`cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:175-192` builds the request from sealed
ResolvedConfig. For bare `ddx work`:

- `Harness` = pt.Harness = "" (empty — no operator pin)
- `Model` = pt.Model = "" (empty — no operator pin)
- `Profile` = `rcfg.Profile()` = `NormalizeRoutingProfile("default")` = `"default"`
- `MinPower` / `MaxPower` = 0 / 0 (defaults)

This is the maximally-permissive shape AC#2 calls for. (Note: the bead
suggests `Profile` could be empty too; however, fizeau accepts `"default"`
as a valid profile — see `fizeau@v0.10.4/service_profiles_test.go:33`,
`internal/routing/engine_test.go:993,1436,1685`.)

### Hypothesis 4 — pre-rejection of "the 4 harnesses"

There is no DDx code that enumerates harnesses and rejects them prior to
calling `Execute`. The only DDx-side `ResolveRoute` callers in the
execution path are:

- `cli/internal/server/workers.go:821` — server worker preflight, gated
  on `m.BeadWorkerFactory == nil && !spec.OpaquePassthrough`
  (`workers.go:803`). For `ddx work`, `OpaquePassthrough=true` is
  propagated via `workerSpec["opaque_passthrough"] = true`
  (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1993-1995`), so this preflight is also skipped.
- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:424` — `ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService`
  orphan-model check, gated on `harnessName == "agent" && model != ""`
  (line 423). Inactive for bare `ddx work`.
- `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go:147` — read-only
  status surface; not in the execution path.

Static guarantees:
- `cli/cmd/routinglint_test.go:69-104` — AST lint asserting non-status
  files in `cmd/` do not call `.ResolveRoute()`.
- `cli/cmd/agent_run_passthrough_test.go:166-198`
  (`TestWorkDoesNotCallResolveRoute`) — runtime assertion: stub
  `ResolveRoute` returns an error; `ddx work --once` succeeds without
  the stub error surfacing.
- `cli/cmd/agent_run_passthrough_test.go:203-230`
  (`TestWorkPassesEmptyHarnessToService`) — runtime assertion: bare
  `ddx work --once` sends `Harness=""` to `Execute`.

All three pass at this rev:
```
$ go test -run "TestWorkDoesNotCallResolveRoute|TestWorkPassesEmptyHarnessToService|TestRoutinglintNonStatusFilesDoNotCallResolveRoute" ./cmd/...
ok      github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/cmd     0.129s
```

## Where the error actually originates

The exact phrase `no viable routing candidate: %d candidates rejected` is
formatted in fizeau's routing engine:

- `~/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/fizeau@v0.10.4/internal/routing/engine.go:258-260`
  ```go
  if len(pins) > 0 {
      return fmt.Sprintf("no viable routing candidate for pins %s: %d candidates rejected", strings.Join(pins, " "), e.Rejected)
  }
  return fmt.Sprintf("no viable routing candidate: %d candidates rejected", e.Rejected)
  ```

The "4 candidates rejected" count corresponds to the four harness candidates
fizeau enumerated and disqualified inside its own `ResolveRoute` call
(invoked from `service.Execute`). DDx never sees those four candidates and
plays no role in their selection or rejection.

## AC mapping

- **AC#1 (identify rejection point)**: no DDx-side rejection point exists
  for bare `ddx work`. Rejection happens inside fizeau's routing engine
  at `fizeau@v0.10.4/internal/routing/engine.go:260`. File:line citations
  for every DDx pre-flight surface that *could* in principle reject are
  enumerated above; each is gated off for the `ddx work` path.
- **AC#2 (DDx pre-flight no longer rejects)**: already true at base rev.
  Bare `ddx work` sends `Harness=""`, `Model=""`, `Profile="default"` to
  fizeau. Verified by `TestWorkPassesEmptyHarnessToService`
  (`cmd/agent_run_passthrough_test.go:203`).
- **AC#3 (structured rejection reasons)**: vacuously satisfied — DDx does
  not produce its own rejections in this path. The server-side preflight
  in `internal/server/workers.go:802-823` does emit a `preflight.rejected`
  event payload (`internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:616-621`) but is
  inactive under `OpaquePassthrough=true`.
- **AC#7 (review existing pre-validation tests)**:
  - `ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService` in `service_run.go:392`: validates
    operator-explicit harness pin only (`harnessName == ""` short-circuits).
    KEEP — validates explicit pin.
  - `routePreflight` in `workers.go:802-823`: validates harness/model
    typed compatibility for non-opaque dispatch paths. KEEP — validates
    explicit pin via fizeau's typed-incompatibility surface; gated off
    for `ddx work` (`OpaquePassthrough=true` skip).
  - No existing tests assert DDx pre-validates auto-routing.

## Recommendation

The DDx-side AC are satisfied at this revision. The operator's reproducer
("bare `ddx work` rejects with no viable routing candidate: 4 candidates
rejected") is a **fizeau routing-engine** failure: fizeau enumerated four
harness candidates and rejected all four, returning the typed
`*routing.NoViableCandidateError` upward through
`Service.Execute → executeOnService` (`service_run.go:198-201`).

A separate bead is needed against fizeau to:
1. Surface per-candidate reject reasons through `ServiceEvent` so DDx can
   log them (today the wrapped error string from fizeau is the only signal
   DDx gets back).
2. Decide whether the "default" profile should pick something when no
   provider/model/harness pin is given on a host with at least one healthy
   harness.

The intersecting bead `ddx-1e516bc9` (fizeau Execute gap) is the natural
home for the fizeau-side investigation and fix. This bead (ddx-9e4c238d)
should be closed: the DDx-side surface area it names is already
maximally-permissive.

## Verification command

```
go test -run "TestWorkDoesNotCallResolveRoute|TestWorkPassesEmptyHarnessToService|TestRoutinglintNonStatusFilesDoNotCallResolveRoute" ./cmd/...
```

Exit 0 ⇒ `ddx work` does not call `ResolveRoute` and forwards an empty
`Harness` to fizeau, satisfying AC#2 / AC#5.

rev 9f7d893b this is not the case for `ddx work`:

3. The exact error string "no viable routing candidate: %d candidates
   rejected" originates inside fizeau at

The investigation report at

each of the bead's four root-cause hypotheses against the code at file:line
